meta.summary: Google shipped Chrome Skills, a new Gemini-powered browser feature that turns reusable prompts into one-click workflows across selected tabs, extending AI automation directly inside Chrome. ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM for more natural real-time voice interaction, while AWS made the Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore generally available to speed enterprise agent deployment. On the research side, the paper "Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models" offers a potentially important recipe for improving post-training efficiency, though it remains early-stage academic work.

items.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
items.summary_short: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms.

topic_refs.headline: "Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models" Proposes a New OPD Training Recipe

items.title: Latent Planning Emerges with Scale
items.summary_short: LLMs can perform seemingly planning-intensive tasks, like writing coherent stories or functioning code, without explicitly verbalizing a plan; however, the extent to which they implicitly plan is unknown.

topic_refs.headline: Latent Planning Emerges with Scale

items.title: Chain-of-Models Pre-Training: Rethinking Training Acceleration of Vision Foundation Models
items.summary_short: In this paper, we present Chain-of-Models Pre-Training (CoM-PT), a novel performance-lossless training acceleration method for vision foundation models (VFMs).

topic_refs.headline: Chain-of-Models Pre-Training: Rethinking Training Acceleration of Vision Foundation Models

items.title: Algorithmic Analysis of Dense Associative Memory: Finite-Size Guarantees and Adversarial Robustness
items.summary_short: Dense Associative Memory (DAM) generalizes Hopfield networks through higher-order interactions and achieves storage capacity that scales as $O(N^{n-1})$ under suitable pattern separation conditions.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

topic_refs.headline: Algorithmic Analysis of Dense Associative Memory: Finite-Size Guarantees and Adversarial Robustness

tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

topic_refs.headline: Aethon: A Reference-Based Replication Primitive for Constant-Time Instantiation of Stateful AI Agents

items.title: A Bayesian Perspective on the Role of Epistemic Uncertainty for Delayed Generalization in In-Context Learning
items.summary_short: In-context learning enables transformers to adapt to new tasks from a few examples at inference time, while grokking highlights that this generalization can emerge abruptly only after prolonged training.

topic_refs.headline: A Bayesian Perspective on the Role of Epistemic Uncertainty for Delayed Generalization in In-Context Learning

items.title: KnowRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning with Minimal-Sufficient Knowledge Guidance
items.summary_short: RLVR improves reasoning in large language models, but its effectiveness is often limited by severe reward sparsity on hard problems.
items.spotlight_comment: RLVR improves reasoning in large language models, but its effectiveness is often limited by severe reward sparsity on hard problems.

items.title: When to Forget: A Memory Governance Primitive
items.summary_short: Agent memory systems accumulate experience but currently lack a principled operational metric for memory quality governance -- deciding which memories to trust, suppress, or deprecate as the agent's task distribution shifts.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

items.title: PipeLive: Efficient Live In-place Pipeline Parallelism Reconfiguration for Dynamic LLM Serving
items.summary_short: Pipeline parallelism (PP) is widely used to partition layers of large language models (LLMs) across GPUs, enabling scalable inference for large models.

items.title: OSC: Hardware Efficient W4A4 Quantization via Outlier Separation in Channel Dimension
items.summary_short: While 4-bit quantization is essential for high-throughput deployment of Large Language Models, activation outliers often lead to significant accuracy degradation due to the restricted dynamic range of low-bit formats.

items.title: M$^\star$: Every Task Deserves Its Own Memory Harness
items.summary_short: Large language model agents rely on specialized memory systems to accumulate and reuse knowledge during extended interactions. Recent architectures typically adopt a fixed memory design tailored to specific domains, such as semantic retrieval for conversations or skills reused for coding.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

items.title: LoSA: Locality Aware Sparse Attention for Block-Wise Diffusion Language Models
items.summary_short: Block-wise diffusion language models (DLMs) generate multiple tokens in any order, offering a promising alternative to the autoregressive decoding pipeline. However, they still remain bottlenecked by memory-bound attention in long-context scenarios.

featured_topics.headline: Google Chrome Skills Launches Gemini-Powered Reusable Prompt Workflows
featured_topics.summary_short: Google AI Blog announced a Chrome capability that turns AI prompts into one-click tools. The post presents this feature through a video showing Skills in Chrome
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Google is productizing browser-native AI automation inside Chrome, signaling a distribution advantage for Gemini in everyday enterprise and consumer workflows.
key_takeaways: Chrome desktop users can save prompts as "Skills" and run them repeatedly across multiple selected tabs, turning ad hoc Gemini usage into standardized browser workflows.
Google is pairing custom user-created Skills with a prebuilt Skill library, which lowers setup friction and could accelerate adoption beyond power users.
The feature extends Gemini's integration inside Chrome rather than requiring a separate app, giving Google a direct path to embed AI task automation into a high-frequency product.

evidence.title: Claude Code’s Source Leaks, OpenAI Exits Video Generation, Gemini Adds Music Generation, LLMs Learn at Inference
Chrome now lets you turn AI prompts into repeatable ‘Skills’

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance Seed Unveils Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM for Real-Time Voice Interaction
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a speech model designed for attentive listening and interference suppression. The model is positioned for more natural interactive voice conversations
featured_topics.why_it_matters: A full-duplex speech model from ByteDance points to intensifying competition in low-latency voice AI, an area with major implications for assistants, customer support, and multimodal agents.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed says Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM is designed for attentive listening, robust interference suppression, and more natural interactive conversations.
The release suggests ByteDance is investing in simultaneous listen-and-speak voice systems rather than turn-based speech pipelines, a key capability for human-like conversational agents.
ByteDance also surfaced Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, indicating an active model release cadence around both speech and foundation-model evaluation.

evidence.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment Opens
Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena

evidence.title: Anthropic announces new Claude Mythos model

featured_topics.headline: "Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models" Proposes a New OPD Training Recipe
featured_topics.summary_short: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms.
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If validated, the paper could improve how frontier labs and model builders distill stronger models into cheaper deployable systems during post-training.
key_takeaways: The paper studies on-policy distillation, a core post-training method for LLMs, and argues that its success depends on two governing conditions rather than simple scaling alone.
Its contribution is mechanistic and prescriptive: it aims to explain OPD dynamics and provide a practical recipe for when distillation should work reliably.
Because the work is currently circulating via arXiv and paper aggregators rather than broad independent replication, it is more relevant for research roadmaps than immediate product decisions.

evidence.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe

featured_topics.headline: Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Reaches General Availability
featured_topics.summary_short: AWS announced general availability of the Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. The open-source SDK is intended to help developers build production-ready AI agents and run them on AgentCore Runtime
featured_topics.why_it_matters: AWS is reducing enterprise adoption friction for agentic AI by connecting Bedrock AgentCore directly into the widely used Spring developer ecosystem.
key_takeaways: The open-source Spring AI AgentCore SDK lets developers build production-ready AI agents in Spring AI and run them on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime.
General availability matters for enterprise buyers because Spring is deeply embedded in Java back-end stacks, making Bedrock easier to adopt without major architectural changes.
The launch strengthens AWS's position in enterprise agent infrastructure by tying model orchestration and runtime services to familiar application-development tooling.

evidence.title: Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is now Generally Available
Best practices to run inference on Amazon SageMaker HyperPod

x_buzz.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
x_buzz.summary: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.
x_buzz.linked_topic: UK gov's Mythos AI tests help separate cybersecurity threat from hype
